sections.about: This metric measures women directors as a percentage of total Board size. It falls under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, Participation/Inclusion/Representation of women; it is not an Essential or Leadership Indicator. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal Stainless leads across all three years: 30.00%, 25.00% and 27.28%. JSW remains structurally strong at 25.00%, 20.00% and 25.00%. Tata is stable at 20.00% in FY2022-23 and FY2023-24 but falls to 10.00% in FY2024-25, while SAIL deteriorates from 17.65% to 12.50% and then 0%. The denominator matters: Tata’s Board stayed at 10, so the fall is entirely numerator-driven; Jindal’s 3 women directors on an 11-member Board produces the best FY2024-25 percentage. Jindal’s FY2024-25 disclosure reports 11 directors, 3 of whom are female, and 6 KMPs with no women KMPs.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 3rd at 10.00%, 63.3% below Jindal Stainless at 27.28%. Tata’s ranking moved from 3rd in FY2022-23 to tied 2nd in FY2023-24, then back to 3rd in FY2024-25. The deterioration is explained by the reduction from 2 women directors to 1, not by board expansion.
